event: Validation: Russian deep-strike defense (1,800km Urals attacks Apr 25) likely understates Ukrainian long-range UAS production ramp — actual monthly production estimated 15,000-25,000 units vs. prior 8,000-12,000 baseline
evidence_basis: Apr 25 2026 UNITED24 article: 'Drones Strike Deep Into Russia's Urals for First Time, Reaching 1,800 Km' — Ukrainian drones struck Yekaterinburg and Chelyabinsk (1,800km from Ukraine). Represents 3x range extension vs. prior Moscow strikes (~500km). Cross-referenced with Ukraine MOD restructuring article (Apr 24) citing 'accelerate battlefield results' under Fedorov.
probability_basis: Strong inference from operational outcome (1,800km strikes require industrial-scale production) and Fedorov's stated acceleration mandate. 0.81 reflects lack of official production figures but credible triangulation from strike tempo and range.
drivers: Ukraine MOD restructuring under Fedorov prioritizing drone production
Deep-strike capability requiring larger airframes (higher motor/ESC/battery demand)
Russian air defense attrition creating target-rich environment
